assets: close exposure to recurring merchant reporting complaints
ability to distinguish real reporting pain from feature requests
likely access to examples of broken reporting workflows
distribution: peer network for discovery and referrals
potential internal champions rather than direct budget holders
network_access: peers in ecommerce SaaS and analytics-related roles
operators adjacent to merchant reporting problems
constraints: network is mostly peers, not founders
wants a micro-SaaS but is unsure whether to start with services
credibility_signals: practical understanding of merchant reporting failures
unfair_advantages: knows which reporting pain repeats enough to monetize
can translate vague reporting complaints into a scoped workflow
can use peer network as discovery and champion layer

opportunities.title: Merchant reporting concierge to automated KPI pack to micro-SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Start with a narrow recurring reporting service, learn which workflow gets budget, then automate the repeated slice into software.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has stronger insight into ecommerce merchant reporting failure modes than a generic builder and can use peers as discovery sources and internal champions.
validation_plan: Pick one narrow buyer context and one fixed outcome
Interview 15 to 20 peers and operators about recurring reporting pain
Run 5 detailed calls focused on the ugliest repeated workflow
Package the repeated pain into a fixed-scope paid pilot
Look for one repeatable delivery unit before building software
tool_specs: Internal intake form for data sources and reporting context
Metric definition map and QA checklist
Template-driven KPI pack and exception summary
Automation for anomaly summarization and standardized output generation
kill_criteria: No recurring reporting pain found after 10 to 15 conversations
No path from peers to a buyer with budget
Work remains fully bespoke with no repeated workflow
No paid pilot or strong pilot commitment from warm outreach

opportunities.title: Productized reporting audit for merchant-facing teams
opportunities.thesis: Sell a fixed-scope audit that identifies broken metrics, confusing dashboards, and missing reporting decision triggers.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can diagnose category-specific reporting problems faster and more credibly than general analytics freelancers.
validation_plan: Define a fixed audit scope and price band
Test warm outreach with peers and adjacent operators
Use sample teardown artifacts to improve conversion
kill_criteria: Prospects only want broad advisory work
The audit does not lead to follow-on engagement or referrals

opportunities.title: Exception-based merchant reporting micro-SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Build a narrow product that detects KPI drift, metric mismatches, or reporting anomalies for one ecommerce reporting job.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user likely knows which reporting exceptions matter, but lacks validated buyer access and willingness to pay for a self-serve tool today.
validation_plan: Use service delivery to identify the single repeated reporting job
Validate budget approval and workflow repetition before product work
tool_specs: Exception alerting or KPI QA engine
Integration with one or two common ecommerce data sources
kill_criteria: Users praise the idea but will not pay
The problem varies too much across accounts
Distribution remains dependent on cold founder outreach

next_actions: Choose one buyer context and one painful reporting deliverable
Run 5 interviews with peers and operators this week
Package the repeated pain into a fixed-scope paid pilot
Use the first 3 to 5 pilots to determine whether the workflow is repeatable enough for micro-SaaS
